Question: Solve the equation $$5 x - 2 y = 10$$ for $$x$$ ⓐ when $$y = 10$$ ⓑ in general ⓒ Which solution is easier for you, ⓐ or ⓑ ? Why?

5x − 2y = 10,

5x − 20 = 10  
5x = 30  
x = 30 ÷ 5 = 6.

So, when y = 10, x = 6.

ⓑ  In general (keep y as a variable)  

5x − 2y = 10  
5x = 10 + 2y  (add 2y to both sides)  
x = (10 + 2y) / 5  (divide by 5)  

x = 2 + (2/5) y.
